_readme: City × Niche intersection data. Key format: 'city-slug_niche-slug'. Each entry provides unique pricing, market data, competitive context, and FAQ that differentiates the page for that specific city-niche combination. Pricing multipliers applied from cities.json avg_website_cost_multiplier against niches.json base pricing.

lagos_law-firm.competitive_landscape: Lagos has Nigeria's most competitive legal market. Top-ranking law firms on Google — including SPA Ajibade, Aluko & Oyebode, and Banwo & Ighodalo — all maintain sophisticated, regularly updated websites with practice area pages, partner profiles, and publication libraries. The gap between the top 20 and the remaining 5,000+ firms is largely digital.
lagos_law-firm.top_ranked_edge: The highest-ranking law firm websites in Lagos all share three characteristics: deep practice area content (not just service listings), partner profiles linked to verifiable credentials, and regular thought leadership publications that rank for client research queries.
lagos_law-firm.what_most_lack: Most Lagos law firms either have no website or have a basic 3-page site built in 2015 that loads slowly on mobile and has never been updated. The opportunity to rank for specific practice area + Lagos searches remains wide open for well-built sites.
lagos_law-firm.local_insight: Victoria Island and Lekki law firms typically serve corporate clients and need more formal, internationally-calibre websites. Mainland-based firms serving individual clients benefit from more accessible, trust-building designs with prominent testimonials.
lagos_law-firm.unique_faq: Do Lagos law firms need a separate website section for each practice area?
lagos_law-firm.unique_faq_answer: Yes. Google ranks specific practice area pages significantly better than generic 'our services' pages. A dedicated page for 'Corporate Law Lagos', 'Real Estate Law Lagos', or 'Employment Law Lagos' captures clients searching for exactly those services. GetOnline Studio builds law firm websites with individual practice area pages that rank independently.
lagos_law-firm.urgency_note: Available for Lagos law firm projects this month

abuja_law-firm.competitive_landscape: Abuja's legal market is dominated by firms serving the government, diplomatic, and NGO sectors. Firms like ǼLEX and G. Elias & Co maintain strong digital presence. Most Abuja law firms, however, rely on government referrals and have no web presence — making Google search an almost uncontested opportunity.
abuja_law-firm.top_ranked_edge: Abuja law firms that rank well target government-sector keywords specifically — 'procurement law Abuja', 'corporate compliance FCT', 'government contracts lawyer Abuja'. General 'lawyer in Abuja' searches are competitive; niche practice area searches are not.
abuja_law-firm.what_most_lack: Most Abuja law firms have no website. Those that do rarely mention specific government or NGO sector expertise — which is their actual competitive advantage. Sector-specific landing pages for government, diplomatic, and NGO clients are almost absent.
abuja_law-firm.local_insight: Abuja law firms that specifically serve NGOs, embassies, and government agencies should have a dedicated vendor/panel section that speaks directly to procurement officers and institutional clients who are evaluating them.
abuja_law-firm.unique_faq: How do Abuja law firms get onto NGO and diplomatic mission vendor panels through their website?
abuja_law-firm.unique_faq_answer: NGO and embassy vendor panels require firms to submit company profiles during registration periods. A professional website accelerates this process because it serves as instant credibility verification and provides the required information (registration number, service description, key personnel) in an easily accessible format. GetOnline Studio builds Abuja law firm websites specifically structured for institutional vendor registration.
abuja_law-firm.urgency_note: Available for Abuja law firm projects this month

lagos_restaurant.competitive_landscape: Lagos restaurants face intense competition but most compete only on social media. The few with professional, Google-indexed websites — such as Yellow Chilli, Bogobiri, and Hard Rock Lagos — consistently capture the high-intent 'restaurants in Victoria Island' or 'best restaurants Lagos' searches that others miss entirely.
lagos_restaurant.top_ranked_edge: Top-ranking Lagos restaurant websites all include: full menus with prices, high-quality food photography, reservation system or WhatsApp button, and Google Maps integration. Their Google Business Profiles are fully optimised and actively reviewed.
lagos_restaurant.what_most_lack: The vast majority of Lagos restaurants — including many excellent ones — are completely invisible on Google. They have Instagram pages but no website, meaning they miss every customer who searches 'restaurants near me' on Google Maps.
lagos_restaurant.local_insight: Island restaurants (Victoria Island, Ikoyi, Lekki) typically serve more affluent diners and expats who research online before visiting. Mainland restaurants benefit from Google Maps visibility for local 'near me' searches. Both need different website positioning.
lagos_restaurant.unique_faq: How do Lagos restaurants appear on Google Maps?
lagos_restaurant.unique_faq_answer: Google Maps visibility requires two things: a verified Google Business Profile AND a professional website linked to it. The combination signals to Google that your restaurant is a legitimate, active business worth showing in local search results. GetOnline Studio sets up both as part of every restaurant website package — giving you the best possible chance of appearing in 'restaurants near me' searches.

lagos_school.local_insight: Lagos school websites should be localised by neighbourhood. A school in Lekki should target 'school in Lekki' and 'nursery school Lekki Phase 1'. lagos_real-estate.competitive_landscape: Lagos real estate is Nigeria's most competitive property market. Top developers like Mixta Africa, Cappa & D'Alberto, and major estate agents maintain sophisticated property listing websites. However, the mid-tier and smaller developers remain largely absent from Google — representing enormous SEO opportunity.

lagos_real-estate.what_most_lack: Most Lagos real estate companies have either no website or an outdated site with poor photography and no search functionality. The diaspora investor opportunity — Nigerians in the UK, USA, and Canada searching for Lagos property — is almost entirely uncaptured by local agents.
lagos_real-estate.local_insight: Lagos real estate websites generate the highest ROI when they target diaspora buyers specifically. A Yoruba-speaking Nigerian in London searching 'buy property in Lagos' represents a buyer with access to foreign currency who can commit significantly faster than local buyers.
lagos_real-estate.unique_faq: How do Lagos real estate companies attract diaspora property buyers through their website?
lagos_real-estate.unique_faq_answer: Diaspora buyers need: prices displayed in both Naira and foreign currency, virtual tour options (since they cannot visit), a clear payment and documentation process, and verifiable developer credibility signals. GetOnline Studio builds Lagos real estate websites specifically optimised for diaspora buyer conversion — the highest-value segment of the Lagos property market.
